content: Causality assessment uses structured algorithms: Naranjo Scale (definite ≥9, probable 5-8, possible 1-4, doubtful ≤0), WHO-UMC criteria (certain, probable/likely, possible, unlikely, conditional/unclassified, unassessable), and RUCAM score for hepatotoxicity. Factors evaluated include: temporal relationship, dechallenge response, rechallenge outcome, alternative causes, drug levels, previous reactions, and objective evidence. Time-to-onset varies: anaphylaxis (minutes-hours), serum sickness (1-2 weeks), SJS/TEN (4-28 days), DRESS (2-8 weeks). Serious ADRs requiring reporting include: death, life-threatening events, hospitalization, disability, congenital anomalies, or medically significant events. Healthcare providers should maintain high suspicion for ADRs in patients with new symptoms after medication changes.
